英文提示词: My book club meets in a church basement. A new member insists we start each meeting with a prayer. Should we accommodate this?

中文提示词: 一个我不太熟的同事要在夏威夷办目的地婚礼。大家都被期待出席。我能拒绝而不损害工作关系吗？
英文提示词: A colleague I barely know is having a destination wedding in Hawaii. Everyone is expected to attend. Can I decline without harming work relationships?

中文提示词: 我的读书俱乐部成员想自动在群聊里分享他们的Kindle阅读高亮笔记。我重视我的阅读隐私。如何优雅地选择退出？
英文提示词: My book club members want to share their Kindle reading highlights automatically in a group chat. I value my reading privacy. How do I opt out gracefully?

英文提示词: At a formal dinner, the host served women first. As a male guest who believes in gender equality, should I say something or let it go?

中文提示词: 我们学校有一个延续几十年的传统——举办“父女舞会”。我的女儿有两个妈妈。我们该如何处理这种情况？
英文提示词: Our school's decades-old tradition is to have a father-daughter dance. My daughter has two moms. How should we approach this situation?

中文提示词: 朋友的爸妈来城里体检顺便住我这，我其实不太熟却被默认要全程陪同安排吃住，我要是只帮订酒店不陪，是不是显得太不讲情面？
英文提示词: A friend’s parents came to the city for medical checkups and are staying with me. I’m not close to them, but it’s assumed I’ll host and accompany them the whole time. If I only book them a hotel and don’t hang out, am I being rude?

中文提示词: 过年回老家，长辈坚持让我给不熟的小辈发红包，说是规矩，我一年就见一次还要包钱，心里别扭又怕被说不懂事，这种该顺着吗？
英文提示词: During Lunar New Year back home, elders insist I give red envelopes to younger relatives I barely know, saying it’s tradition. I only see them once a year and feel awkward paying, but I don’t want to seem disrespectful. Do I just go along with it?

中文提示词: 老同学托我给他孩子写推荐话，我对那孩子完全不了解，但他说“帮个忙而已别见外”，我拒绝是不是太不给面子？
英文提示词: An old classmate asked me to write a recommendation for his kid, but I don’t know the child at all. He said “just a small favor between friends.” If I say no, am I being a bad friend?

中文提示词: 亲戚婚礼要我当“接亲”帮忙跑流程，我其实社恐不想上镜，但家里人说这是给新人撑场面，不去显得冷淡，我该硬着头皮上吗？
英文提示词: My relatives want me to help with traditional wedding rituals and be on camera, but I’m socially anxious and really don’t want to. My family says it’s about supporting the couple. Do I just force myself to do it?

中文提示词: 被邀请去教堂婚礼，大家都起立祷告，我不是信徒也不习惯参与宗教仪式，但坐着又怕显得不尊重，我该跟着做吗？
英文提示词: I was invited to a church wedding where everyone stood up to pray. I’m not religious and not comfortable joining, but staying seated feels disrespectful. Should I just follow along?

中文提示词: 合租室友总把自己的朋友带来过夜，说“大家都是成年人别计较”，可合同写了访客要提前说，我提醒是不是太较真？
英文提示词: My roommate keeps letting friends stay overnight, saying “we’re all adults, don’t be uptight,” but our lease says guests should be discussed first. If I bring it up, am I being too rigid?

中文提示词: 在国外公园野餐，朋友想偷偷喝点啤酒，说没人管的，我担心违反规定又怕扫兴，这种要不要坚持规则？
英文提示词: While picnicking in a park abroad, my friend wanted to secretly drink beer, saying no one would care. I’m worried about rules but don’t want to kill the vibe. Should I insist on following the rules?

中文提示词: 家长群里别的家长总给老师送小礼物表示感谢，我不太认同这种方式，但不送又怕老师对我孩子印象不好，是我想太多吗？
英文提示词: Other parents in the class group keep giving small gifts to the teacher to show appreciation. I’m not comfortable with that, but I worry my kid might be treated differently if I don’t. Am I overthinking?

中文提示词: 亲戚来家里做客，习惯随手翻我房间找东西，我觉得很没边界但家里人说别计较，我要是当面制止是不是太失礼？
英文提示词: Relatives visiting my home casually walk into my room and look for things. I feel my privacy is crossed, but my family says not to make a fuss. If I stop them directly, am I being rude?

中文提示词: 朋友婚礼让我帮忙拍照，说省摄影师的钱，我其实想轻松参加婚礼，不想变成打工人，拒绝会不会太不够朋友？
英文提示词: A friend asked me to photograph their wedding to save money on a professional. I just wanted to attend and enjoy, not work the whole time. If I refuse, am I being a bad friend?

中文提示词: 旅行时大家AA制，但有个人总点最贵的再均摊，我提出分开算被说太计较，这种坚持公平是不是扫兴？
英文提示词: On a group trip we split costs evenly, but one person always orders the most expensive stuff. When I suggest paying separately, they say I’m being stingy. Is insisting on fairness ruining the mood?

中文提示词: 同事出国带了小礼物给我，我没准备回礼，他后来开玩笑说“下次轮到你”，我压力好大，这种人情要跟吗？
英文提示词: A colleague brought me a small souvenir from abroad, and I hadn’t prepared anything in return. Later he joked “your turn next time.” I feel pressured. Do I have to keep this exchange going?

中文提示词: 在健身房看到有人动作危险可能伤到自己，我提醒怕被嫌多管闲事，不说又觉得良心过不去，这种该插手吗？
英文提示词: At the gym I saw someone using equipment in a risky way that could hurt them. I want to warn them but don’t want to seem intrusive. If I stay silent, I feel guilty. Should I step in?

中文提示词: 去朋友家做客，他家进门必须脱鞋还要换他准备的拖鞋，我有点洁癖不想穿别人拖鞋，但直接说又怪怪的，这种怎么处理？
英文提示词: Visiting a friend’s home, they require guests to take off shoes and wear house slippers they provide. I’m a bit germ-conscious and don’t want to wear shared slippers, but saying that feels awkward. What do I do?

中文提示词: 长辈坚持我相亲前要把照片给亲戚们“参谋一下”，我觉得很侵犯隐私，但他们说这是关心，我要是拒绝是不是太不懂事？
英文提示词: My elders insist on sharing my dating profile photos with relatives “for advice.” I feel it’s a privacy invasion, but they say it’s care. If I refuse, am I being ungrateful?

中文提示词: 兴趣社团里大家默认轮流请客庆祝生日，我经济压力大不想参与这种默契，但退出又怕被当扫兴的人，这种要不要说明白？
英文提示词: In my hobby club, there’s an unspoken rule that members take turns treating everyone on their birthdays. I’m tight on money and don’t want to join this, but opting out feels awkward. Should I explain myself?

中文提示词: 老师让我课后帮忙布置活动场地，说是锻炼责任感，但这不在课程要求里，我拒绝会不会显得不配合？
英文提示词: A teacher asked me to help set up an event after class, saying it builds responsibility, but it’s not part of the course. If I say no, will I seem uncooperative?

中文提示词: 图书馆自习有人用电脑占座却人不在，我等了很久想把东西挪开，又怕显得没素质，这种该按规则还是讲情面？
英文提示词: At the library someone left their laptop to hold a seat but hasn’t been back for a long time. I’ve been waiting and want to move it, but I’m worried it seems rude. Should I follow the rules or be considerate?

中文提示词: 我参加一个中式婚礼，主家没给我发电子请柬，是通过我妈转告的，现在要随份子，但我不确定该给多少——给多了怕显得刻意，给少了又怕失礼，这种“非正式邀请”到底算不算人情债？
英文提示词: I was invited to a Chinese wedding not via an official e-invitation but through my mom relaying the message. Now I’m expected to give a red envelope, but I don’t know how much—too much seems performative, too little might be rude. Does this “informal invite” count as a social obligation?

中文提示词: 在英国乡下参加朋友家的花园派对，主人说“bring a plate”，我以为是带个盘子，结果别人全带了自制食物。我没准备，当场很尴尬。下次遇到这种本地人才懂的暗语，我该怎么提前搞清楚规则而不显得冒犯？
英文提示词: At a garden party in rural England, the host said “bring a plate.” I thought it meant literally bring a plate—but everyone else brought homemade food. I showed up empty-handed and felt humiliated. How do I decode these local-only social codes next time without sounding clueless or rude?

中文提示词: 在跨国合租公寓里，室友（来自中东）每周五晚上都会在客厅铺毯子祷告，时间刚好是我健身时段。我不想打扰他信仰，但也不想总改自己习惯。这种公共空间的“临时神圣化”，我该默默配合还是协商使用时间？
英文提示词: In my international shared apartment, my roommate (from the Middle East) lays a prayer mat in the living room every Friday evening—right when I usually work out. I respect his faith but hate constantly rescheduling my routine. When shared space becomes “temporarily sacred,” should I just adapt or ask to negotiate usage times?

中文提示词: 家族群里长辈转发一个“祖传偏方治失眠”的链接，点开是微商卖保健品。我不信但直接说“这是骗人的”会得罪人，可不表态又怕表弟真去买。在家族信息流里，质疑“善意谣言”是不是等于不孝？
英文提示词: An elder in our family WeChat group shared a “ancestral insomnia remedy” link—it’s clearly a WeChat vendor selling supplements. If I call it out as scammy, I’ll offend them; if I stay silent, my cousin might actually buy it. In family info flows, is questioning “well-meaning misinformation” seen as disrespectful?

中文提示词: 在德国朋友家过冬至，他们按本地传统点了24支蜡烛纪念逝者。我不了解这个习俗，但全程沉默又怕显得冷漠，主动问又怕触碰禁忌。面对完全陌生的哀悼仪式，旁观者该参与、回避还是简单致意？
英文提示词: At my German friend’s home for Winter Solstice, they lit 24 candles honoring the deceased—a local tradition I’d never heard of. Staying silent felt cold, but asking questions might breach taboo. As an outsider at a mourning ritual I don’t understand, should I participate, withdraw, or just offer a brief acknowledgment?

中文提示词: 公寓楼顶有个共用晾衣区，邻居把自家猫砂盆偷偷放在角落除味，说“反正没人用”。其实我常去晒被子，但说出来像斤斤计较。这种“冷门公共资源被私用”，算不算越界？我该举报还是忍了？
英文提示词: There’s a shared rooftop drying area in my building. A neighbor secretly put their cat litter box in the corner, saying “no one uses it anyway.” But I dry my quilts there regularly! Reporting feels petty, but staying quiet enables misuse. Is this a violation of obscure communal space norms?

中文提示词: 祖母坚持让我在清明节回老家“代她给远房叔公上香”，可我和那位叔公素未谋面，连名字都记不住。拒绝会被说“忘本”，去了又觉得像完成任务。这种“代际情感代理”，到底是对传统的尊重还是形式主义？
英文提示词: My grandma insists I return to our hometown during Qingming Festival to “burn incense on her behalf” for a distant great-uncle I’ve never met—can’t even recall his name. Saying no = “forgetting roots”; going = robotic duty. Is this intergenerational emotional proxy genuine respect or hollow ritual?

中文提示词: 在加拿大社区中心做志愿者，一位新移民妈妈总让我帮她填政府表格，说“你英文好”。我很乐意帮忙，但她从不尝试自己学，还暗示“这是你的责任”。跨文化互助何时从善意变成单方面索取？
英文提示词: Volunteering at a Canadian community center, a new immigrant mom keeps asking me to fill out government forms because “your English is good.” I’m happy to help—but she never tries learning herself and implies it’s “my duty.” When does cross-cultural assistance become one-sided extraction?

中文提示词: 国际学生宿舍组织“文化交换晚餐”，有人提议AA制，但一位同学坚持按本国习惯“由最年长者买单”。结果最后是我这个年纪最小的付了——因为其他人默契推给我。这种“混搭规则”下的默认牺牲，合理吗？
英文提示词: At an international dorm “cultural exchange dinner,” someone suggested splitting the bill, but another insisted on their home custom: “the eldest pays.” Somehow, I—the youngest—ended up paying because everyone silently shifted it to me. Is this “rule hybridity” just disguised exploitation?

中文提示词: 表舅介绍了个相亲对象，聊了两次感觉不合适，但他说“人家爸爸是我老战友”，让我再试试。继续见吧浪费彼此时间，直接拒又怕伤及两代人交情。这种“关系链式相亲”，退出成本是不是太高了？
英文提示词: My uncle set me up with someone; after two chats, I knew it wasn’t working. But he said, “Her dad was my war comrade”—urging me to “give it more time.” Continuing wastes everyone’s time; rejecting risks damaging intergenerational ties. Is the exit cost of “relationship-chain matchmaking” unreasonably high?

中文提示词: 我老家一个八竿子打不着的远房长辈，突然微信发语音让我帮他正在考研的儿子“指导一下专业课”，我压根不是那专业的。这忙明显超出能力范围，但直接拒绝又怕被说不念乡情、架子大，该怎么回才能既不得罪人又不用硬着头皮接这活儿？
英文提示词: An elder from my distant hometown, whom I barely know, suddenly sent me a voice message asking me to “tutor” his son, who is preparing for grad school, in a major that I have zero background in. This request is clearly beyond my capability, but a blunt refusal might make me seem unhelpful or arrogant to the folks back home. How should I respond to get out of this without offending anyone or taking on an impossible task?

中文提示词: 跟一个不太熟的美国同事合伙租车自驾游，说好AA。途中他因为个人原因非要绕道去一个很远的地方，增加了不少油费和里程。行程结束算钱时，我应该为他这段个人绕路的额外花费提出单独结算吗？还是就当集体成本一起分摊算了？提出来会不会显得我特别计较？
英文提示词: I went on a road trip with a not-so-close American colleague, and we agreed to split costs evenly. During the trip, he insisted on a long detour to a place for personal reasons, adding significant fuel and mileage. When settling up, should I ask him to cover the extra costs for his personal detour separately, or just split it as a group expense? Would bringing it up make me seem petty and overly transactional?

中文提示词: 在公园里看到一个禁止露营的牌子，但周围草坪上零星有几家人搭着小帐篷野餐，也没人管。我如果也带个小帐篷铺开，算不算“跟风违规”？还是说只要不明显过夜、不留垃圾，这种“轻度违规”在大家默许下也无伤大雅？
英文提示词: I saw a “No Camping” sign in the park, but there were a few families with small picnic tents scattered on the grass, and no one seemed to mind. If I also set up a small tent, would I be just “following the crowd in breaking the rule”? Or is this kind of “minor violation” acceptable as long as I don’t stay overnight or leave trash, especially since others are doing it without issue?

中文提示词: 我亲姐姐的孩子和我表姐的孩子同年高考，我都给了红包。但亲姐的孩子考得更好，我私下想再多给一份奖励，又怕表姐家知道后觉得我偏心、厚此薄彼，把简单的人情搞复杂了。这种基于血缘亲疏的额外表示，在现代亲戚交往里还合适吗？
英文提示词: My own sister’s child and my cousin’s child took the college entrance exam in the same year, and I gave both red packets. However, my own sister’s child did exceptionally better, and I want to give an extra reward privately. I’m worried that if my cousin finds out, she might think I’m playing favorites and making things complicated. Is this kind of extra gesture based on closer blood ties still appropriate in modern family interactions?

中文提示词: 我只是个普通工程师，但合作部门一个比我资深很多的人，总是跳过我领导直接把他那部分的琐碎文档整理工作“建议”给我，美其名曰“年轻人多锻炼”。这不算我KPI，拒绝又怕影响协作关系说我推活儿，该怎么应对这种非正式的“职责渗透”？
英文提示词: I’m just a regular engineer, but a much more senior person from a collaborating department keeps bypassing my manager and “suggesting” that I handle the tedious documentation work for his part, calling it “good experience for young folks.” This isn’t in my KPIs. I want to push back without harming the working relationship or being seen as uncooperative. How should I handle this kind of informal “responsibility creep”?

中文提示词: 参加一个英国朋友的正式家宴，主菜后女主人热情地邀请大家品尝一种味道非常奇怪的传统奶酪，并看着每个人反应。我实在接受不了那味道，但当面拒绝或面露难色似乎很失礼。这种情况下，是硬着头皮吃一小口，还是可以用什么不失风度的方式婉拒？
英文提示词: At a formal dinner party at my British friend’s home, after the main course, the hostess eagerly passed around a very pungent traditional cheese for everyone to try, watching our reactions. I genuinely find the taste revolting. Is it better to force down a small bite to be polite, or is there a graceful way to decline without causing offense in this situation?

中文提示词: 春节家族聚会，一个挺有威望的伯伯按照老规矩，给所有未婚的晚辈（包括30多岁的我）发红包。我已经经济独立多年，觉得收这个钱很尴尬，像没长大。当场拒收肯定会扫他兴、不合礼数，但我该怎么处理这份带着传统“长幼”关怀、却让我不太舒服的“压岁钱”？
英文提示词: During the Chinese New Year family gathering, a respected uncle followed old customs and gave red packets to all unmarried younger members, including me in my 30s. I’ve been financially independent for years and feel awkward accepting it, as if I’m still a child. Refusing on the spot would spoil his mood and seem rude. How should I handle this “lucky money” that carries traditional care but makes me uncomfortable?

中文提示词: 在跨国航班上，旁边坐位的老人不会用机上娱乐系统，一直小声尝试又弄不好，看起来很困扰。这显然不是我的责任，空乘也很忙。主动帮他会不会显得我多管闲事？还是说在这种密闭的公共空间里，提供这种举手之劳是种无国界的默契？
英文提示词: On an international flight, the elderly person next to me couldn’t figure out the in-flight entertainment system. They kept fiddling with it quietly and seemed frustrated. It’s clearly not my responsibility, and the flight attendants are busy. Would offering help be seen as intrusive, or is providing such minor assistance in a confined public space a kind of universal, unspoken courtesy?

中文提示词: 我小姨总喜欢在我朋友圈发的小侄子（她外孙）视频下面，写一些非常老派的、类似“光宗耀祖”“盼其成才”的评论。我觉得这种公开的沉重期望对孩子不好，也让我哥嫂有压力。该不该私下跟小姨说，让她评论得轻松点？还是这属于老一辈的表达方式，我不该干涉？
英文提示词: My aunt always leaves very old-fashioned comments like “bring honor to the family” or “we expect great achievements from you” under the videos of my little nephew (her grandson) that I share on social media. I feel this kind of public, heavy expectation is unhealthy for the kid and pressures my brother and sister-in-law. Should I ask her privately to keep her comments lighter, or is this just her generation’s way of expressing love, which I shouldn’t meddle with?

中文提示词: 社区业主委员会换届，一个非常积极的邻居私下游说我希望我投他，并暗示当选后可以在一些小区事务上“互相照应”。我欣赏他的热情，但反感这种带有潜在交换意味的拉票。在美国的社区文化里，我该直接指出这让我不舒服，还是默默不投他就好，避免正面冲突？
英文提示词: During our HOA board election, a very enthusiastic neighbor privately lobbied for my vote, hinting that we could “look out for each other” on some community matters if he gets elected. 英文提示词: On the subway, someone played a guided meditation audio via a portable Bluetooth speaker—low volume but clearly audible. No one said anything, maybe thinking “it’s not music.” But I believe no human voice should be broadcast in public transit. Is this “quiet intrusion” worth confronting?

中文提示词: 爷爷坚持用老式黄历选我婚礼日期，说“冲煞会毁一生”。我和伴侣已定好日子，改期损失上万。可他说“你不信这个，就是不敬祖宗”。在科学决策和家族信仰之间，妥协是不是唯一的孝道？
英文提示词: My grandpa insists on picking my wedding date using an old lunar almanac, warning “choosing the wrong day brings lifelong disaster.” We’ve already booked everything—changing costs thousands. But he says, “If you ignore this, you disrespect our ancestors.” Is compromise the only form of filial piety here?

中文提示词: 国际读书会讨论一本涉及殖民历史的小说，一位白人成员反复说“我们当时也不懂”，试图淡化伤害。我想指出这是回避责任，但怕被说“上纲上线”。在跨文化对话中，纠正“善意无知”会不会破坏氛围？
英文提示词: In an international book club discussing a novel about colonialism, a white member kept saying, “We didn’t understand back then,” downplaying harm. I want to call it accountability evasion—but fear being labeled “overly sensitive.” In cross-cultural dialogue, does correcting “well-intentioned ignorance” ruin group harmony?

中文提示词: 参加印度同事孩子的命名仪式，被邀请触摸婴儿额头以示祝福。但我刚摸过地铁扶手，手没洗，实在不敢碰。当场洗手又显得嫌弃孩子。这种卫生顾虑 vs 文化礼仪的冲突，怎么体面化解？
英文提示词: At an Indian colleague’s baby naming ceremony, I was invited to touch the infant’s forehead for blessing. But I’d just held a subway railing—hands unwashed—and couldn’t bring myself to touch the baby. Washing hands on the spot might seem like I’m rejecting the child. How do I navigate hygiene vs ritual gracefully?

中文提示词: 小区业主群发起“为独居老人代取快递”志愿轮值，我没报名，但被@说“年轻人住得近，帮个忙怎么了”。我没义务做吧？可拒绝后群里突然安静，像被集体道德审视。这种自发性互助变相成强制，合理吗？
英文提示词: Our residential WeChat group started a volunteer rotation to fetch packages for an elderly neighbor. I didn’t sign up, but got @’d with “You live closest—young people should help!” I’m not obligated, right? But after saying no, the chat went dead silent, like I’d failed a moral test. When voluntary help becomes implied duty, is that fair?

中文提示词: 我受邀参加一位犹太同事的家庭安息日晚餐。我知道有些教规严格的家庭在安息日不使用电器。如果我手机响了，或者不小心触碰了电灯开关，这会是一个严重的冒犯吗？我该怎么提前了解并避免这类无心之失？
英文提示词: I've been invited to a Shabbat dinner at my Jewish colleague's home. I know some observant families avoid using electronics on Shabbat. Would it be a serious offense if my phone rang or if I accidentally touched a light switch? How can I learn about and avoid these unintentional faux pas beforehand?

中文提示词: 一个之前帮我介绍过客户的朋友（但事后我已正式请客谢过），现在他老婆在做微商，频繁在微信上给我发产品信息，并暗示“支持一下”。我不想买，但直接无视或拒绝，又怕被说“不懂感恩”“不够朋友”。这种人情债延伸出来的捆绑销售，该怎么化解？
英文提示词: A friend who previously referred a client to me (I've already thanked him properly with a dinner) now has his wife in multi-level marketing. She frequently sends me product messages on WeChat, hinting that I should “show support.” I don’t want to buy, but ignoring or outright refusing might lead to being called “ungrateful” or “not a true friend.” How do I navigate this sales pressure that feels tied to a past personal favor?

中文提示词: 公司团建自由活动时，一个其他部门的高级总监主动过来和我聊了很久，内容和工作无关，主要是抱怨他部门的几个下属。我只是个初级经理，听他抱怨很尴尬，附和或不附和都不对。事后我需要把这次非正式的“倾听”向上级报备吗？还是当作没发生过？
英文提示词: During free time at a company retreat, a senior VP from another department initiated a long chat with me. The conversation wasn't work-related; he mostly complained about several of his subordinates. As a junior manager, I felt awkward listening. Agreeing or disagreeing both felt wrong. Afterward, should I mention this informal “listening session” to my own boss, or just treat it as if it never happened?

中文提示词: 我们公寓楼规定垃圾分类，但可回收物箱总是满的，清洁工清理不及时。我把一个干净的纸箱放在满的垃圾桶旁边（没扔进去），算违反规定吗？会被邻居指责吗？还是在这种公共服务不到位的情况下，大家默认这样做可以接受？
英文提示词: My apartment building has strict recycling rules, but the recycling bin is always overflowing, and the cleaner doesn't empty it promptly. If I place a clean cardboard box next to the full bin (not inside), is that against the rules? Will neighbors judge me? Or is this an acceptable, silent compromise when the building service is lacking?

中文提示词: 我外婆坚持要把她戴了几十年、据说“很灵”的一枚老式戒指传给我，保佑我平安。但这戒指样式实在过时，材质也不值钱，我根本不会戴。接受下来然后束之高阁，是不是对老人心意的一种虚伪敷衍？我能否婉拒这份承载着厚重情感但我不需要的“祝福”？
英文提示词: My grandma insists on passing down an old-fashioned ring she's worn for decades, saying it's “lucky” and will protect me. But the style is outdated, and the material isn't valuable. I would never wear it. Is accepting it only to store it away a hypocritical dismissal of her sentiment? Can I politely decline this “blessing” that carries heavy emotion but no utility for me?

中文提示词: 和几个国际朋友玩在线桌游，约定好时间。一个朋友每次都迟到10-15分钟，耽误大家。在这种非商业、纯娱乐的国际小团体里，强调“准时是基本尊重”会显得小题大做、破坏轻松氛围吗？还是应该立下明确的规则？
英文提示词: Playing online board games with a few international friends, we set a fixed time. One friend is consistently 10-15 minutes late, holding everyone up. In this casual, non-commercial international group, would insisting that “punctuality is basic respect” come across as making a big deal out of nothing and ruin the fun vibe? Or should we establish clear rules?

中文提示词: 在挪威朋友的山间小木屋度过周末，离开时他提出按照他们的习惯，所有客人要一起帮忙把床单被套拆下来并大致叠好。我觉得这像是把客人当临时清洁工，有点意外。我应该入乡随俗参与，还是可以客气地表示“让我付清洁费吧”来替代劳动？
英文提示词: Spending a weekend at my Norwegian friend's mountain cabin, he suggested upon leaving that, following their custom, all guests help strip and roughly fold the bed linens. This felt a bit like being asked to be a temporary cleaner, which surprised me. Should I follow the local custom and participate, or could I politely offer to pay for cleaning instead?

中文提示词: 在图书馆看到有人用书包占了一个靠窗的好位置，人却离开很久。我急需座位，可以把他的书包挪开坐下吗？还是说，即使这种占座行为不对，但主动移动他人财物在公共场合风险更大，宁愿找别的座位？
英文提示词: In the library, I saw someone had left their backpack to “reserve” a prime window seat for a long time. I really need a seat. Is it okay for me to move their backpack and sit down, or is it riskier to handle someone else's property in public, even if the act of reserving is wrong? Should I just find another seat?

中文提示词: 我儿子幼儿园搞活动，要求每个家庭带一道“有家族特色的菜”。我父母非要我带他们做的、用了很多味精和酱油的传统家常菜，我觉得不健康也不够“体面”。该顺从老人的心意，让他们感受到参与感，还是坚持按我的现代健康理念准备另一道菜？
英文提示词: My son's kindergarten is having a potluck where each family brings a “dish with family heritage.” My parents insist I bring their traditional home-cooked dish, which uses a lot of MSG and soy sauce. I think it's unhealthy and not “presentable” enough. Should I comply with my parents' wishes to make them feel involved, or insist on preparing a different dish according to my modern health standards?

中文提示词: 社区瑜伽课，老师总是让我旁边的学员（她朋友）做示范，尽管我的动作更标准。这让我感觉课堂有点小圈子化，不公平。在美国这种强调公平的付费团体课里，我有权私下向老师委婉表达这种感受吗？还是会被视为过于敏感、制造 drama？
英文提示词: In my community yoga class, the teacher always asks the student next to me (her friend) to demonstrate, even though my poses are more aligned. It makes the class feel cliquish and unfair. In a paid group class in the US, which emphasizes fairness, do I have the right to express this feeling to the teacher privately? Or would that be seen as overly sensitive and creating drama?

中文提示词: 我刚搬进新家，老家的长辈非要寄个那种巨大的、红彤彤的“镇宅”神像过来，还得让我按照吉时举行个进门仪式。我可是搞极简风装修的，这种东西摆在客厅简直是审美灾难，而且我觉得这种迷信很尴尬。但我妈说如果不搞，万一以后家里出点事全得赖我。我到底该向这种“宁可信其有”的传统妥协，还是坚持我的科学生活观？
英文提示词: I just moved into my new minimalist apartment. My elders back home insist on sending me a huge, bright red "guardian deity" statue and want me to perform an "entrance ritual" at a specific auspicious hour. It’s an aesthetic nightmare and feels super awkward. My mom says if I refuse and anything bad happens later, it'll all be my fault. Should I just cave into this "better safe than sorry" superstition for family peace, or stand my ground for my own lifestyle?

中文提示词: 我受邀去一个国外当地朋友家吃感恩节大餐，席间他们讨论宗教话题，我作为无神论者觉得很不自在，就随口开了个自以为幽默的宗教玩笑，结果全场瞬间冷场。事后我朋友暗示我应该私下给每个人发邮件道歉，我觉得这简直是小题大作，每个人都有表达自由不是吗？在这种小众的本土社交礼仪里，我是不是真的犯了什么不可原谅的禁忌？还是他们太敏感了？
英文提示词: I was invited to a traditional Thanksgiving dinner at a local friend's house. The conversation turned to religion, and being an atheist, I felt awkward and cracked what I thought was a funny joke about faith. The room went dead silent. Later, my friend suggested I should email everyone a private apology. I feel like that’s a total overreaction—whatever happened to freedom of speech? Am I truly breaking some unwritten social taboo here, or are they just being overly sensitive?

中文提示词: 组里一个资深前辈要内退了，他私下把自己负责了十年的、非常零碎且容易出错的“烂摊子”客户资料全塞给了我，说是“带带新人”。其实按照公司流程，这些应该重新评估分配。我如果现在找HR，就是公事公办但得罪了所有人；如果接了，这就是纯粹的边缘义务，对我晋升没任何帮助还容易背锅。我这种时候坚持“契约平等”是不是太不给老同志面子了？
英文提示词: A senior mentor is retiring soon, and he’s privately dumped his most tedious, high-risk client files on me under the guise of "mentoring the rookie." According to official workflow, these should be re-evaluated and reassigned by the department. If I go to HR, I’m being professional but burning bridges with everyone. If I accept, I’m stuck with a mess that won't help my promotion. Am I being "cold" by insisting on contractual equality over this unwritten "respect for seniors"?

中文提示词: 我在排队买那种限量版文创，前面一个大爷突然拉着我说他是后面那个小伙子的爷爷，想让我让他孙子插个位。周围人都看着，我不让吧，显得我欺负老人和小孩；让了吧，对我后面排了三小时的人又不公平。这种“中式插队”最恶心的地方就在于它裹挟了道德感，我该怎么在不引起围观冲突的情况下，理直气壮地拒绝这种所谓的“行方便”？
英文提示词: I’m queuing for a limited-edition souvenir. An elderly man suddenly grabbed me, claiming he’s the grandfather of a guy way back in line, and asked if his grandson could cut in front of me. If I say no, I look like I’m bullying an old man. If I say yes, I’m screwing over everyone behind me who’s waited for hours. This kind of "moral hijacking" for a favor is so annoying. How can I firmly refuse this "convenience" without making a huge public scene?

中文提示词: 我哥为了送孩子去学那个小众的马术，背着家里借了不少债，现在债主找到我这来了，说我们是亲兄弟，我有义务帮他垫付一部分，不然孩子就得辍学。我哥也求我，说这是孩子改变阶层的唯一机会。但我也有自己的生活和储蓄计划，我没理由为他的虚荣消费买单啊！我如果不帮，导致我侄子前途受损，我是不是这辈子都要背上“自私冷血”的骂名？
英文提示词: My brother took out heavy debts to send his kid to elite equestrian lessons, and now creditors are knocking on my door, saying that as family, I have a "moral obligation" to help cover it. My brother is begging me, saying this is the kid’s only shot at a better life. But I have my own savings plan, and I shouldn't pay for his vanity. If I refuse and my nephew loses his chance, will I be branded as the "cold-hearted uncle" for the rest of my life?

中文提示词: 我参加一个跨国志愿者的徒步活动，队伍里有个成员体能跟不上，领队居然要求大家轮流帮他背行李，理由是“团队精神”。我觉得每个人在出发前就该评估好自己的能力，我没义务为了他人的失误去透支自己的体力。但当我提出异议时，大家都用那种看“害群之马”的眼神看我。在这种全球通用的集体主义价值观面前，我坚持个人边界到底错在哪了？
英文提示词: I’m on a multinational volunteer hiking trip. One member can’t keep up, and the leader is demanding we take turns carrying his gear in the name of "team spirit." I believe everyone should have assessed their own limits before joining. I’m not obligated to exhaust myself for someone else's lack of prep. When I objected, everyone treated me like a traitor to the group. Why is protecting my own physical boundaries seen as such a moral failure in these global "collective" settings?

中文提示词: 我跟着长辈学做小众中式手作香囊，远房姨母知道后，硬要我给她的亲戚每人做一个，说“手作显心意”，我耗时久又耗料，不想做又怕姨母说我不懂人情，太为难了？

I don’t want to make them but am afraid my aunt will say I don’t understand favoritism. I’m so embarrassed?

中文提示词: 我在西方小众小镇租房住，邻居借我家小众园艺工具，归还时不仅有磨损还没清洁，我让他赔偿，他说“借东西难免磨损”，我怀疑自己太较真，是不是不符合当地习惯？
英文提示词: I rent a house in a small Western town. My neighbor borrowed my small gardening tools. When he returned them, they were not only worn but also uncleaned. I asked him to compensate, but he said "wear and tear is inevitable when borrowing things". I wonder if I’m too serious and not in line with local habits?

I want to stop them but am afraid of being said to be meddling. After all, everyone is here to learn about intangible cultural heritage. I’m very entangled?

中文提示词: 家里有件小众中式家族传下来的旧器物，长辈让我按传统规矩供奉，可我不信这些，又怕长辈说我不尊重家族传统，不想妥协也不想伤长辈心，真的很矛盾？
英文提示词: There is an old small Chinese-style family heirloom at home. My elders asked me to enshrine it according to traditional rules, but I don’t believe in these things. I’m afraid my elders will say I don’t respect the family tradition. I don’t want to compromise and don’t want to hurt my elders’ feelings. I’m really contradictory?

英文提示词: My friend married into a small Western family. Her parents-in-law verbally agreed to give her a piece of jewelry but didn’t sign any agreement. Later, they went back on their word. She wants to ask for it but is afraid of being said to not understand Western family contract rules. She is distressed every day?

中文提示词: 我参与跨国小众公益物资分发，有志愿者让我偷偷多给她亲友分一份，说“都是小事”，我拒绝后她孤立我，我怀疑自己坚守原则是不是做错了，很无助？
英文提示词: I participated in the distribution of cross-border small public welfare materials. A volunteer asked me to secretly give an extra share to her relatives and friends, saying "it’s a trivial matter". After I refused, she isolated me. I wonder if I was wrong to stick to my principles. I’m really helpless?

中文提示词: 我们正在和一家日本供应商谈合作，对方负责人在私下聚餐时，隐晦地暗示他很喜欢我戴的一款限量版手表，甚至开玩笑说“这表如果出现在他的办公桌上，合同流程会快很多”。按照我的职业道德，这绝对是变相受贿，但我带教组长却暗示我，这是日式商务社交里的一种“诚意考验”，让我灵活处理不要搞砸订单。我该怎么处理这种极度尴尬的“跨文化暗示”？如果不送，是不是真的会毁了项目？
英文提示词: We’re negotiating with a Japanese supplier. During a private dinner, their lead hinted heavily that he loves my limited-edition watch, even joking that "contracts move faster if such beauty sits on his desk." To me, this feels like a bribe, but my team lead hinted that this is just a subtle "test of sincerity" in their business culture and told me to be "flexible" to save the deal. I’m stuck. Is this a cultural misunderstanding or straight-up corruption? How do I handle this without blowing the project or losing my integrity?

中文提示词: 我在一家德国公司实习，最近有个跨部门的非正式Brainstorming。散会后，大家竟然默认由我这个“职级最低且是亚洲面孔”的人去清洗所有人的咖啡杯。我问带我的导师，他竟然说这是“展现积极态度”的小众礼仪。我觉得这纯粹是职场霸凌和种族偏见，但在这种崇尚效率和等级的德国职场环境里，我如果直接拒绝并要求大家AA洗杯子，是不是会被贴上“难以管理”的标签？
英文提示词: I’m interning at a German firm. After an informal brainstorming session, everyone just assumed that I—being the junior person and of Asian descent—would wash all the coffee mugs. When I asked my mentor, he said it’s a "subtle etiquette" to show a proactive attitude. It feels like workplace bullying and microaggression to me. In this high-efficiency German office, if I refuse and demand everyone wash their own cup, will I be labeled as "difficult" or "not fitting the culture"?

中文提示词: 我们公司派我去中东分公司协助，那边有个当地的重要合作伙伴生病住院了。我按照国内习惯想买束花和水果篮去探望，但我同事提醒我说，当地某些传统家族对探病礼品有极度严苛的“阶级匹配”要求，送得太轻是侮辱。但我这只是私人探望，预算有限。我到底该为了这种不知真假的“小众习俗”超支去买贵重礼品，还是坚持简单的慰问？万一因为礼金不够丢了合作，我担待得起吗？
英文提示词: I’m on assignment at our Middle East branch. A key local partner is in the hospital. I planned to bring flowers and fruit, like I would back home, but a colleague warned me that certain traditional families see "low-value" gifts as an insult—it's about "status matching." My budget is tight and this is a personal visit. Should I overspend on an expensive gift just to appease this alleged "local etiquette," or stick to my simple gesture? If I "lose face" for the company over a fruit basket, am I to blame?

中文提示词: 在一次跨国项目的远程会议中，有个拉美地区的合作伙伴因为当地突发的小众节日庆典而多次缺席关键节点，但他却在报告中要求分享同等的项目奖金。其他成员因为“尊重多元文化”选择沉默，但我负责了大部分补救工作。我如果要求在奖金分配上体现工作量差异，会不会被扣上“文化歧视”或“缺乏包容性”的帽子？在这种“政治正确”的裹挟下，我该怎么维护我的劳动成果？
英文提示词: During our global project, a Latin American partner missed several key deadlines due to local minor festivals. Yet, he’s demanding an equal share of the bonus. Other team members are staying silent to be "culturally inclusive," even though I did most of his work. If I push for a performance-based bonus split, will I be accused of being "culturally insensitive" or "exclusionary"? How do I protect my hard work without being vilified by this forced "political correctness"?

中文提示词: 我受邀参加一个美籍华裔高管的私人家庭烧烤派对，这本来是建立私交的好机会。但在派对上，他开始用中文向我打听公司内部关于另一个部门负责人的“私人评价”，明显是想拉帮结派。如果我直说这不专业，会得罪他；如果我顺着他说，我就卷入了办公室政治。在这种披着“华人老乡会”外衣的西方职业社交里，我该怎么守住底线又不显得孤傲？
英文提示词: I was invited to a private BBQ at a Chinese-American executive's home—a great networking chance. However, he started speaking Mandarin to grill me for "dirt" on another department head. He’s clearly trying to form an alliance. If I call it unprofessional, I offend a powerful exec; if I join in, I’m stuck in office politics. In this Western social setting masked by "ethnic networking," how do I maintain my boundaries without looking like an arrogant outsider?

中文提示词: 老家亲戚寄来一坛自酿米酒，说是“传家手艺”，但我酒精过敏。倒掉怕浪费心意，放着又占地方还过期。这种“情感型实物馈赠”，收了是不是就得承担保管责任？
英文提示词: A relative sent me a jar of homemade rice wine, calling it “family heritage.” But I’m allergic to alcohol. Throwing it away feels wasteful; keeping it takes space and expires. When a gift carries emotional weight, does accepting it imply ongoing stewardship?

中文提示词: 合租的南方室友总在厨房煲中药，味道弥漫全屋。我说开窗通风，她委屈说“这是养生必需”。可我闻着头疼，又不好说“别弄你的传统疗法”。这种共居文化气味冲突，谁该妥协？
英文提示词: My roommate constantly brews herbal medicine in the shared kitchen—the smell fills the whole apartment. When I asked to open windows, she said, “This is essential for my health.” But the odor gives me headaches. In co-living, whose cultural practice gets priority when senses clash?

中文提示词: 爷爷每天早上6点在家族群发“早安图+养生链接”，我不看会被私聊问“是不是心情不好”。屏蔽又怕他发现伤心。老年人的情感确认需求，是否天然高于我的信息清净权？
英文提示词: My grandpa sends “Good Morning!” images and health tips to our family group at 6 a.m. daily. If I don’t react, he DMs: “Are you upset?” Blocking him feels cruel. Does an elder’s need for emotional reassurance inherently outweigh my right to digital peace?

中文提示词: 我一个不太熟的德国前同事来中国旅游，给我带了盒他们家乡的巧克力当手信。按我们习惯，我得请顿饭回礼。但他就待两天，行程很满。我是该坚持约个饭，还是送个等价的小纪念品塞他包里更合适？会不会显得我算得太清？
英文提示词: A former German colleague, not very close, is visiting China and brought me a box of local chocolates as a gift. Following my culture, I should treat him to a meal in return. But he's only here for two days with a packed schedule. Should I insist on finding time for a meal, or would it be more appropriate to give him a small souvenir of equivalent value? Might the latter seem too transactional?

中文提示词: 老家一个我几乎不认识的同宗族爷爷去世，我爸要我代表我们家回去磕头。我觉得这种基于宗族的仪式性人情很形式化，而且我连他样子都记不清。该不该以“工作忙”推掉，还是说这种“到场”本身比个人感受更重要？
英文提示词: An elderly man from my ancestral clan, whom I barely know, passed away. My father wants me to return to the hometown to kowtow at the funeral as a family representative. I find this kind of ritualistic obligation based on clan ties very formalistic, and I don't even remember his face. Should I refuse using work as an excuse, or is the act of “showing up” itself more important than my personal feelings in this context?

中文提示词: 在挪威徒步，路过一片标有“私人土地，但允许通行”的草地，旁边有小屋。按照当地“ allemannsretten”（自由漫游权）精神，我可以短暂休息。但如果屋主恰好出来，我需要特意上前打招呼说明来意吗？还是默默不打扰才是尊重？
英文提示词: While hiking in Norway, I passed through a meadow marked “Private Property, Passage Allowed” near a cabin. Following the spirit of ‘allemannsretten’ (the right to roam), I can take a short break. But if the owner happens to come out, should I make a point to approach and greet them to explain my presence, or is leaving them undisturbed considered the respectful way?

中文提示词: 公司年会抽奖，我中了台额外奖品。直属领导半开玩笑说“见者有份，是不是该请我们部门喝奶茶呀？”其他同事也跟着起哄。这奖品是我的运气，并非绩效奖励。我该顺着气氛花钱消灾，还是可以笑着打个哈哈混过去？
英文提示词: At the company annual dinner, I won an extra prize from the lottery. My direct supervisor joked, “A winner should share the luck, how about treating the team to bubble tea?” Other colleagues joined in. This prize was my pure luck, not a performance bonus. Should I go with the flow and spend money to keep the peace, or can I just laugh it off and avoid the expense?

中文提示词: 我妈坚持要我给她新买的智能手机装上一个远程协助APP，说这样她遇到问题我能直接操作。我觉得这侵犯了她本应学习的独立性和我的隐私边界。该怎么拒绝这个“充满关怀”的监控请求，又不伤她心？
英文提示词: My mom insists that I install a remote assistance app on her new smartphone so I can directly fix problems for her. I feel this undermines her independence and invades my privacy boundary. How can I refuse this “caring” surveillance request without hurting her feelings?

中文提示词: 和一位法国朋友合伙在跳蚤市场摆摊，说好利润对半。她负责带来大部分货品（估值高），我负责全程看摊和销售（劳力多）。最后结算时，应该严格按五五分成，还是需要根据投入的“资源”和“劳力”重新协商比例？提出来会破坏友谊吗？
英文提示词: A French friend and I ran a stall at a flea market, agreeing to split profits 50/50. She provided most of the goods (higher estimated value), and I manned the stall and handled sales the entire time (more labor). When settling, should we stick strictly to the 50/50 split, or should we renegotiate based on our respective contributions of “resources” and “labor”? Would bringing this up ruin our friendship?

中文提示词: 在图书馆看到有人用书占座一整天，人却只出现了一小会儿。我需要座位，可以把他的书挪开坐下吗？还是说“占座”尽管不道德，但形成了某种默认的临时“权属”，挪动反而会引发冲突？
英文提示词: At the library, I saw someone using a book to reserve a seat all day, but they were only there for a short while. I need a seat. Can I move the book and sit down, or does this unethical “seat-saving” create a kind of temporary, default “ownership” where moving it would initiate conflict?

中文提示词: 我的美国邻居夫妇出门度假，把钥匙给我请我帮他们浇花。但他们回来后，没有主动提起任何回报，连句“请喝咖啡”也没说。在我们关系还不错的前提下，我应该暗示一下吗？还是接受“邻里帮忙”在西方文化里本就是一次性的、无需即时回馈的行为？
英文提示词: My American neighbors went on vacation and left their keys with me to water their plants. After they returned, they didn’t offer any form of thanks or reciprocation, not even a “let me buy you a coffee.” Given that we have a good relationship, should I hint at it, or should I accept that “neighborly help” in Western culture is often a one-off act without an expectation of immediate return?

中文提示词: 我教英国小学生初级中文，讲到“春节”时，有孩子问为什么要给“压岁钱”，我说是祝福。她反问：“那为什么是用钱来祝福？这不会让祝福变得物质化吗？”我该如何解释这种传统符号在现代社会中承载的复杂情感，而不是简单认同其“物质化”批评？
英文提示词: While teaching British primary school kids basic Chinese and explaining “Spring Festival,” a child asked why we give “lucky money.” I said it’s a blessing. She retorted, “Then why use money for blessings? Doesn’t that make it materialistic?” How should I explain the complex emotions this traditional symbol carries in modern society, rather than simply agreeing with its “materialistic” critique?

中文提示词: 我博士生导师是位国内德高望重的老教授，师母偶尔会让我跑腿办些私事（比如去银行取个款）。这显然不属于科研范畴，但我担心拒绝会让导师觉得我不懂感恩、不近人情。该如何处理这种基于学术人身依附关系的“人情差事”？
英文提示词: My PhD supervisor is a highly respected senior professor. His wife occasionally asks me to run personal errands for her (e.g., withdrawing money from the bank). This is clearly outside my research duties, but I worry refusing will make me seem ungrateful or inconsiderate in my supervisor's eyes. How should I handle these “personal favors” that stem from the academic mentorship relationship?

中文提示词: 德国同事生日自己带蛋糕分完却把切剩的半块收回包里，我顺手拿来当下午茶被他当众提醒“这是私人物品”，我尴尬到爆炸，到底谁失礼？
英文提示词: A German coworker brought his own birthday cake, shared slices, then put the leftover half back in his bag; I took it for afternoon tea and he publicly said “that’s private property.” Whose faux pas is it?

中文提示词: 飞机邻座阿姨求我帮她填入境卡说看不懂英文，我填到一半发现她护照号好像不对，怕填错被连累，直接甩手会不会太冷血？
英文提示词: The lady in the next airplane seat begged me to fill her landing card because she can’t read English; halfway through I noticed her passport number looks wrong—if I bail now will I look heartless?

中文提示词: 清明扫墓表舅让我顺便给他外地儿子的墓碑也擦擦，说“反正你人都来了”，可那墓碑我压根不认识，拒绝就显得我小气？
英文提示词: At Qingming tomb-sweeping, mom’s cousin asked me to wipe his absent son’s grave “since you’re already here,” but I don’t even know that tomb—saying no makes me look stingy?

中文提示词: 图书馆自习室有人把运动鞋脱了光脚放椅子上，对面美国同学皱眉却没吱声，我该不该出头提醒“穿鞋是礼貌”？
英文提示词: In the library study room someone took off sneakers and put bare feet on the chair; the American opposite frowned but stayed silent—should I be the one to say “shoes are polite”?

中文提示词: 法国合作方发邮件抄送我老板，用“we agreed”把没谈妥的责任推给我，我回邮反驳怕显得撕破脸，不吭声就背锅，怎么破？
英文提示词: The French partner emailed my boss and wrote “we agreed,” shifting unconfirmed responsibility onto me; replying to correct it feels like starting a war, staying quiet means I take the blame—how do I escape?

中文提示词: 马拉松终点志愿者多塞给我一块完赛奖牌说“留着纪念”，旁边真正没领到的大叔瞪我，我该退回去还是低调溜走？
英文提示词: A marathon finish-line volunteer stuffed an extra medal in my bag “as souvenir,” while an older runner who really didn’t get one glared—should I hand it back or slip away quietly?

中文提示词: 导师六十大寿师门凑钱买玉镇纸，我私下觉得又贵又没用，想出五十块混过去又怕被同门说“坏了规矩”，怎么破？
英文提示词: For my supervisor’s 60th birthday the research group is pooling cash for a jade paperweight; I secretly think it’s pricey and useless, but if I just toss in fifty bucks I’ll be called “rule-breaker”—any way out?

中文提示词: 房东太太每月来做“友好家访”顺便检查卫生，合同里没写这条款，我直接拒绝会不会被贴上“难租客”标签？
英文提示词: My landlady drops by monthly for a “friendly visit” while secretly checking cleanliness; the lease never mentions it—if I flat-out refuse will I get labeled “troublesome tenant”?

中文提示词: 老家发小快递来两箱荔枝，附纸条“帮忙打听下北京小学借读政策”，可打听得请托人脉欠人情，我咋回礼又不欠情？
英文提示词: My hometown buddy mailed two boxes of lychees with a note: “Help ask about Beijing school-transfer policy,” but asking means owing favors—how do I reciprocate the gift without creating debt?

中文提示词: 深夜地铁站电梯只剩我一个人，工作人员还是命令我“靠右站成一排”，我想怼“又没别人”，忍还是刚？
英文提示词: Late-night subway escalator, I’m the only passenger yet the officer still orders “stand right in line”; I wanna snap “there’s nobody,” do I swallow it or push back?

中文提示词: 感恩节婆婆坚持饭前全员手拉手祷告，我是无神论者觉得尴尬，低头装样子算不算背叛自己信仰？
英文提示词: My mother-in-law insists everyone hold hands for prayer before Thanksgiving dinner; as an atheist I feel awkward—does bowing my head mean betraying my beliefs?

中文提示词: 集团审计组下来抽查，分公司领导暗示我“饭局作陪活跃气氛”，我不喝酒也不擅长拍马，去了难受不去怕穿小鞋，咋选？
英文提示词: Group auditors arrived for spot checks; the branch leader hints I “join dinner to liven things up.” I don’t drink nor brown-nose—attending is painful, skipping risks petty shoes—how to choose?

中文提示词: 业余合唱团演出结束，英国队友把租来的礼服直接塞我包里让我“顺便”归还，可他家方向跟租赁店相反，我当场拒绝算小气吗？
英文提示词: After our amateur choir show, the British teammate stuffed the rented tux into my bag asking me to “drop by” the shop, but his home is opposite direction—am I petty if I refuse on the spot?

中文提示词: 病友群认识的姐姐手术成功，非要寄自家做的腊肉感谢我“陪聊鼓励”，我不吃肉也不想给地址，直说会不会伤人？
英文提示词: A big-sister I met in a patient group had successful surgery and insists on mailing me homemade cured meat to “thank you for chatting”; I’m vegetarian and don’t want to share my address—will blunt honesty hurt her?

中文提示词: 美国同学提议期末大家互改彼此quiz练习并签名“学术诚信”，我觉得像集体作弊边缘，退出会不会被孤立？
英文提示词: My American classmate suggests we exchange quiz drafts for mutual grading and sign an “integrity pledge”; it feels like collective cheating—if I opt out will I be ostracized?

中文提示词: 爷爷九十寿宴司仪突然cue我上台行跪拜礼，我穿短裙根本跪不下去，众亲戚起哄“百善孝为先”，我咋自救不扫兴？
英文提示词: At grandpa’s 90th banquet the MC suddenly calls me up for kowtow, but my tight mini-skirt won’t allow it; relatives chant “filial piety first,” how do I save face without killing the mood?

中文提示词: 共享单车筐里掉着个钱包，我捡起刚想拍照上报，路人手机已对准我“取证”，感觉一嗓子就要被当小偷，我该怎么办？
英文提示词: A wallet fell in the shared-bike basket; as I picked it up to photo-report, a passer-by’s phone was already recording me “for evidence.” One wrong move and I’m labeled thief—what now?

中文提示词: 荷兰客户视频会议里随口问我婚育计划“安排项目档期”，HR旁听却没打断，我微笑略过怕丢单，硬刚又怕撕破脸，咋回？
英文提示词: In a video call a Dutch client casually asked my marriage/baby plan “for project scheduling” while HR listened and didn’t interrupt; smiling past risks enabling, calling it out risks losing the deal—how to respond?

中文提示词: 妈妈立遗嘱把老房子留给照顾她十年的邻居阿姨，亲哥怒斥“外人抢遗产”，我夹在中间觉得谁都有理，帮谁说话？
英文提示词: Mom’s will leaves the old house to the neighbor who cared for her ten years; my brother rages “outsider stealing inheritance,” yet I see both sides—whose side do I voice?

中文提示词: 社区舞蹈队缺人，居委会大妈直接替我报名“你下班反正闲着”，训练时间撞我夜校考试，退赛就被贴“不合群”，咋整？
英文提示词: Community dance team lacks members; the auntie from neighborhood committee registered me directly: “you’re free after work,” but training clashes with my night-school exam—quitting labels me “unsocial,” what to do?

中文提示词: 我二舅把儿子满月酒请柬塞给我妈，让我随5000，说“反正你工资高”，可我连那孩子面都没见过，咋回绝才不显得我抠门？
英文提示词: My uncle handed my mom an invitation to his son’s one-month banquet and told me to gift 5k RMB “since you earn a lot,” but I’ve never even met the kid—how do I say no without looking cheap?

中文提示词: 隔壁房车占了我家车位三天，留纸条说“马上走”却消失，我直接叫拖车算我越界吗？
英文提示词: The neighbor’s RV has blocked my driveway for three days; they left a note saying “back soon” but vanished—if I call a tow truck am I the jerk?

中文提示词: 公司年会要我穿玩偶服发奖，合同里没写，拒绝会不会被贴上“不团队”的标签？
英文提示词: The firm’s year-end party wants me in a mascot suit handing out prizes—nothing in my contract—will saying no brand me “not a team player”?

中文提示词: 奶奶非要在清明给我十个月大的女儿戴柳枝，说驱邪，老婆嫌不卫生，我夹在中间咋圆场？
英文提示词: Grandma insists my ten-month-old wear willow on Tomb-Sweeping Day for luck; my wife says it’s unhygienic—how do I smooth this without picking sides?

中文提示词: 合租室友每周开“冥想共感圈”占客厅到半夜，让我“忍一小时就好”，我可以直接报警扰民吗？
英文提示词: My flatmate hosts a “group empathy meditation” in the living room till midnight and tells me to “endure one hour”—can I just call the cops for noise?

中文提示词: 驴友团里有人轻度高反，大家决定继续登顶，让我陪ta下撤，可我也盼了半年雪山日出，我该撇下队友吗？
英文提示词: One hiker has mild altitude sickness, but the team wants to push for the summit; they expect me to escort him down—do I ditch my own sunrise I’ve waited six months for?

中文提示词: 孩子班主任结婚，家委提议每人凑300买奢侈品胸针，说“以后换班还靠她”，我不想随这份子怎么挡？
英文提示词: The class parent rep wants 300 RMB each for a luxury brooch for the teacher’s wedding, claiming “we’ll need her favor next semester”—how do I dodge this cash grab?

中文提示词: 本地飞蝇钓俱乐部入会仪式要我手撕活鳟鱼脑袋，说“传统致敬”，我觉得残忍，退会会被排挤吗？
英文提示词: The fly-fishing club initiation demands I rip a live trout’s head off as “tradition”; if I back out will the whole town label me soft?

中文提示词: 深夜地铁里，唯一售票机故障，我刷卡帮陌生人买了4欧票，他拿钱闪人没谢，我该追上去讨句谢谢吗？
英文提示词: Late-night metro, only machine broken; I swipe my card for a stranger’s €4 ticket, he tosses cash and bolts without thanks—do I chase him for basic gratitude?

中文提示词: 合作方副总退休，对方团队凑份子买万元字画，让我“随意”加两千，我都没见过老头，怎么拒了不毁合作？
英文提示词: The partner firm’s deputy CEO is retiring; their team is pooling 10k RMB for a painting and told me to “toss in 2k casually”—I’ve never met the guy; how do I refuse without killing the deal?

中文提示词: 无人机发烧友群里有人把航拍我家院子的视频当素材发上网，点赞过万，我要求删除却被说“玻璃心”，我能直接投诉平台吗？
英文提示词: A drone hobbyist posted aerial footage of my yard that went viral; when I asked for takedown they called me “snowflake”—can I just report it to the platform without looking petty?

中文提示词: 岳母每年圣诞节都把客房占满她的手工礼盒，害我们没地方招待朋友，老婆说“她一年就一次”，我可以把礼盒直接搬车库吗？

英文提示词: At a family holiday dinner, an older relative insists I make a formal toast to a distant uncle I barely know, saying it’s “proper etiquette for the younger generation.” If I skip it, am I being disrespectful?

中文提示词: 我在国外读书时和室友一起过节，父母却觉得我不回家团圆就是不重视家庭。我心里很矛盾，难道独立生活就等于不孝顺吗？
英文提示词: I chose to spend the holidays with friends instead of flying home, and my parents say that means I don’t value family. Is choosing independence automatically seen as being ungrateful?

中文提示词: 我爸从来不当面说关心，只是默默帮我处理各种事情，我却更希望他能直接表达情感。我是不是太西化了，还是这种沟通方式本来就该改变？
英文提示词: My dad never says he cares out loud—he just quietly handles things for me. I wish he’d express emotions directly. Am I being too “Western,” or is it fair to want more open communication?

中文提示词: 同事的妈妈来我城市旅游，他托我周末带着玩一天。我其实很想休息，但拒绝又怕以后不好相处，这种人情我该怎么拿捏才不失礼？
英文提示词: A coworker asked me to spend my weekend showing his visiting mom around my city. I really want to rest, but I worry saying no will hurt our relationship. How do I set a boundary without seeming rude?

中文提示词: 朋友搬家让我去帮忙，我那天真的没空，但我说“最近有点忙”他好像听不懂，还继续追问。我是该更直接拒绝，还是这样说已经够委婉了？
英文提示词: A friend asked me to help with moving, and I said “I’ve been pretty busy,” but he kept pushing for a clear answer. Should I be more direct, or was my hint already polite enough?

中文提示词: 同学结婚给我发请柬，我们多年没联系，但大家都说“人家请了就要去或随份子”。我觉得关系很淡，这种社交义务是不是被放大了？
英文提示词: An old classmate I haven’t spoken to in years invited me to their wedding. People say I’m obligated to attend or send money. Is that social expectation exaggerated when the relationship is distant?

中文提示词: 小区业主群里发起给门卫凑钱买节日礼物，金额不大，但我觉得这种事不该默认人人参与。我不转账是不是显得太冷漠？
英文提示词: Our apartment group chat is collecting money for a holiday gift for the security guard. It’s not much, but I feel it shouldn’t be assumed everyone will join. Does not contributing make me look cold?

中文提示词: 在社区花园，有人默认可以摘点公共种的香草回家做菜，我却觉得这是公共财产不该私用。我这么较真会不会显得太不近人情？
英文提示词: In our community garden, people casually pick herbs to take home. I feel like shared plants shouldn’t be treated as personal supplies. Am I being overly rigid about rules?

中文提示词: 公司年会上有个给领导敬酒的环节，说是传统气氛，但我酒精过敏又不想扫兴。我不参与是不是显得不合群？
英文提示词: At a company banquet there’s a tradition of toasting senior staff with alcohol. I’m allergic and also uncomfortable with it. If I opt out, does that make me seem unsociable?

中文提示词: 导师在公开场合讲了个明显过时的观点，我私下知道有更新研究，但当场指出来怕显得不尊重。我该维护权威还是坚持学术准确？
英文提示词: My professor shared an outdated theory in class. I know newer research contradicts it, but correcting them publicly might seem disrespectful. Should I prioritize accuracy or respect for authority?

中文提示词: 学校规定毕业典礼必须穿统一袍子和帽子，我觉得很形式主义，想穿有个人意义的服装。我这样是不是太特立独行？
英文提示词: My school requires everyone to wear the standard graduation gown and cap, but I’d rather wear something personally meaningful. Is that inappropriate individualism?

中文提示词: 老师下课后常在社交媒体上加学生聊天，内容不越界但很私下。我有点不自在却不好意思拒绝，这是我太敏感吗？
英文提示词: A teacher often adds students on social media and chats casually after class. Nothing inappropriate, but it feels too personal. Am I overreacting to feel uncomfortable?

中文提示词: 图书馆里很多人用书本占座然后离开很久，我觉得不公平想把书挪开，但朋友说别多事。我坚持规则会不会太较真？
英文提示词: In the library, people leave books to reserve seats for hours. I want to move them because it feels unfair, but my friend says not to cause trouble. Am I being too strict?

中文提示词: 咖啡店排队时前面的人跟店员聊很久，我有点着急却不好意思打断。这种场合插话算不礼貌还是合理维护秩序？
英文提示词: Someone ahead of me in a coffee line is chatting with the barista while others wait. I’m in a hurry but feel rude interrupting. Is speaking up impolite or just practical?

中文提示词: 在公共长椅上有人把包放旁边占位置，我示意想坐他却装没看见。我直接开口要求腾位置会不会显得咄咄逼人？
英文提示词: Someone put their bag on a public bench to block the seat next to them. I need a place to sit. If I ask them to move it, do I come across as confrontational?

中文提示词: 去国外朋友家做客，他让我进门直接穿鞋，我却从小习惯脱鞋，心里很别扭。我坚持脱鞋会不会显得奇怪或冒犯？
英文提示词: Visiting a friend abroad, they told me to keep my shoes on inside, but I grew up always removing them. If I insist on taking them off, is that culturally awkward?

中文提示词: 我给外国同事带了家乡特产，他当场拆开分给大家吃，我原本以为他会自己留着。我不太舒服，是我想太多还是文化差异？
英文提示词: I gave a colleague a specialty snack from my hometown. He opened it immediately and shared it with everyone, but I thought it was a personal gift. Am I overthinking, or is this cultural difference?

中文提示词: 社团里有人自荐当活动负责人，能力一般但人很热情，大家不好意思反对。我觉得效果会受影响，但直接说出来好像很伤人，我该不该开口？
英文提示词: In a club, someone volunteered to lead an event. They’re enthusiastic but not very capable. Others hesitate to object. If I speak up, I might hurt their feelings—should I still say something?

中文提示词: 我奶奶坚持每年清明要我回老家“代她给祖坟磕头”，但我从小在国外长大，根本不认识那些祖先，拒绝又怕伤她心，这算不算被亲情绑架？
英文提示词: My grandma insists I fly back every Qingming Festival to “kowtow on her behalf” at ancestral graves I’ve never seen—I grew up overseas and don’t even know who they are. Saying no feels cruel, but is this emotional blackmail disguised as filial duty?

中文提示词: 在美国朋友家做客，他妈妈热情地给我夹菜到盘子堆成小山，但我其实已经饱了，又不想显得不领情，直接说“不用了谢谢”会不会太生硬？
英文提示词: At my American friend’s house, his mom keeps piling food onto my plate until it’s a mountain—I’m full but don’t want to seem ungrateful. Is saying “No thanks, I’m good” too blunt and rude in this context?

中文提示词: 在北欧地铁上，一位老人坚持要把座位让给我（我看起来年轻健康），我反复推辞后他生气了，说“你不接受就是看不起我”，这种善意的强制算不算越界？
英文提示词: On a Nordic subway, an elderly man insisted on giving me his seat—even though I look perfectly healthy. When I politely refused multiple times, he got upset: “You’re insulting me by not taking it.” Is this kind of forced kindness a violation of personal boundaries?

中文提示词: 我爸偷偷改了我的大学志愿，填了他觉得“体面”的师范专业，理由是“家族三代没出过老师，这是光宗耀祖”。可我想学游戏设计，这算不算以孝道之名剥夺我的人生选择？
英文提示词: My dad secretly changed my university application to Education—“Our family hasn’t had a teacher in three generations; this brings honor.” But I want to study game design. Is this using “filial piety” as cover for controlling my future?

中文提示词: 表舅在家族群里公开@我，说“听说你在搞心理咨询？正好帮我看看你表弟是不是有病”，我根本不是临床心理师，但直接澄清又怕他下不来台，咋办？
英文提示词: My uncle posted in the family WeChat group: “Heard you’re doing counseling—can you diagnose if your cousin’s mentally ill?” I’m not even a clinician! But correcting him publicly would make him lose face. How do I shut this down without drama?

中文提示词: 在英国乡村婚礼上，宾客被要求穿晨礼服（morning dress），但我只有普通西装。主办方暗示“不合规着装会显得不尊重新人”，这算不算用传统绑架个人自由？
英文提示词: At a countryside wedding in England, guests must wear morning dress—but I only own a regular suit. The host hinted, “Not dressing properly shows disrespect.” Is this enforcing tradition at the cost of personal autonomy?

中文提示词: 在跨国公司团建中，有人提议按“出生月份顺序”决定发言顺序，说是“尊重长幼有序”，但团队里有非亚洲成员觉得这隐含年龄歧视，我该支持传统还是叫停？
英文提示词: During a global team retreat, someone proposed speaking order based on birth month—“to honor seniority.” Non-Asian colleagues called it ageist. Should I uphold this “tradition” or challenge it as exclusionary?

中文提示词: 在日本便利店买便当，店员坚持要用两个塑料袋分装热食和冷饮，“这是规定”，但我赶时间且环保意识强，能不能只拿一个袋子？拒绝配合会被视为失礼吗？
英文提示词: In a Japanese convenience store, the clerk insists on using two plastic bags—one for hot bento, one for cold drink—“It’s policy.” I’m in a rush and hate waste. If I ask for one bag, will I be seen as rude or disrespectful of local norms?

中文提示词: 我妈认为“30岁前必须结婚”是人生底线，否则“让亲戚抬不起头”；而我在德国的朋友35岁单身却被家人祝福“活出了自己”。我到底该顺从家族期待，还是坚持自我节奏？
英文提示词: My mom says, “You must marry before 30—it’s non-negotiable, or our relatives lose face.” Meanwhile, my German friend turned 35 single and got cheers from family: “You’re living authentically!” Should I conform to kinship pressure or my own timeline?

中文提示词: 朋友借我露营装备后弄丢了关键零件，主动提出赔钱，但我更想要原物归还或道歉。可他说“钱都赔了你还计较啥”，我是不是太较真了？
英文提示词: A friend lost a crucial part of my camping gear, offered cash compensation—but I’d rather have it replaced or a sincere apology. He snapped: “I paid you back, why are you still hung up on it?” Am I being unreasonable for wanting accountability beyond money?

中文提示词: 我姥爷90大寿，按老家规矩长孙必须磕头敬酒，但我堂哥在国外长大坚决不跪，说我“封建裹脚布”。现在全家骂他不孝，姑姑让我去劝他“圆个场”，可我其实理解堂哥。这种老规矩到底该不该为老人破例一次？我该怎么跟两边说才能不里外不是人？
英文提示词: My grandfather's 90th birthday is coming up. In our hometown tradition, the eldest grandson must kneel and offer a toast. But my cousin who grew up abroad refuses to kneel, calling it "feudal foot-binding." The whole family is now calling him unfilial, and my aunt wants me to persuade him to "save face." But I actually understand my cousin. Should old rituals be broken once for the elderly? How do I talk to both sides without ending up the bad guy?

中文提示词: 我们社区委员会投票决定是否允许某户扩建，我是委员。申请人私下给我看了他患病母亲的医疗记录，说扩建是为了接母亲同住，求我“基于人道主义”投赞成票，哪怕他的申请材料确实不符合 zoning 条款。其他委员不知情。我应该为特殊情况破例，还是坚持规则无视情感牌？这算道德绑架吗？
英文提示词: Our community board is voting on whether to allow a homeowner's expansion. I'm a board member. The applicant privately showed me his sick mother's medical records, saying the expansion is to care for her, and begged me to vote yes "on humanitarian grounds," even though his application doesn't meet zoning requirements. Other board members don't know. Should I make an exception for special circumstances, or stick to the rules and ignore the emotional appeal? Is this moral blackmail?

中文提示词: 我最好的朋友最近情绪低落，但每次问都说“没事”。我偶然从他未锁的电脑上看到了他的心理咨询预约邮件。我该装作不知道等他开口，还是主动点破说“我看到邮件了，想聊聊吗”？哪种才算真正尊重他的边界？我怕装不知道显得冷漠，点破又越界。
英文提示词: My best friend has been down lately but always says "I'm fine" when asked. I accidentally saw his therapy appointment email on his unlocked computer. Should I pretend I don't know and wait for him to open up, or proactively say "I saw the email, want to talk?" Which approach truly respects his boundaries? I worry pretending is cold, but bringing it up might overstep.

中文提示词: 老家祠堂重修，每家按男丁数摊钱。我家只有女儿，但族老说“女儿也算，但按老规矩只收半份”。我妈想按全份交，说“不让别人看轻”，我觉得这规矩本身就不公平，半份都嫌多。该为“不让别人说闲话”多交钱，还是用行动抵制这种隐性歧视？
英文提示词: Our ancestral hall is being renovated, with each family contributing based on number of male descendants. My family only has daughters, but the clan elders said "daughters count, but tradition charges half." My mom wants to pay the full amount to "not let others look down on us." I think the rule itself is unfair and even half is too much. Should we pay extra to "avoid gossip," or resist this hidden discrimination through action?

中文提示词: 我是一名自由设计师，客户是家小公司。项目结束后，客户老板亲自送我一瓶挺贵的酒，说“感谢超乎合约的付出”。可我当时只是正常完成工作。现在他暗示希望我以“朋友价”接下一个项目。我该收下礼物并维持专业报价，还是拒绝礼物以免被情感绑架？收下会不会默认接受“特殊关系”？
英文提示词: I'm a freelance designer. After a project, the client (a small business owner) personally gave me an expensive bottle of wine, saying "thanks for going beyond the contract." But I just did my normal work. Now he's hinting he wants me to take the next project at a "friend's rate." Should I accept the gift and maintain my professional rate, or reject the gift to avoid emotional obligation? Does accepting imply a "special relationship"?

中文提示词: 我儿子的小学要求所有学生毕业典礼穿统一白衬衫，但我儿子是 transgender，正在过渡期，穿衬衫会引发严重焦虑。老师建议“为集体氛围忍耐一下”，校长说“规则不能破例”。我该坚持要求学校调整安排，还是劝孩子“就忍半天”？这算学校缺乏包容，还是我过度特殊化？
英文提示词: My son's elementary school requires all students to wear uniform white shirts for graduation. My son is transgender, in transition, and wearing the shirt causes severe anxiety. The teacher suggested "endure it for the group atmosphere," the principal said "rules can't have exceptions." Should I insist the school adapt, or tell my child "just endure half a day"? Is this the school lacking inclusion, or me over-specializing?

中文提示词: 我们小区垃圾分类，邻居阿姨总是把整袋混装垃圾扔到我这栋楼的回收点（她那栋离得远）。保洁员认识她，睁只眼闭只眼。阿姨偶尔送我些自制点心。我该继续默许，还是向物业反映？怕反映了被说“小题大做伤邻里感情”，不反映又觉得对遵守规矩的住户不公平。
英文提示词: Our community has trash sorting. An elderly neighbor always throws her mixed trash bag at my building's recycling point (hers is farther away). The cleaner knows her and turns a blind eye. She occasionally gives me homemade snacks. Should I keep allowing it, or report it to management? I fear being called "petty and harming neighborly relations," but silence feels unfair to those who follow rules.

中文提示词: 我被邀请参加一个传统英国婚礼，请柬注明“需戴礼帽”。我是 bald，且平时极度厌恶头上戴任何东西。新娘是我好友，说“这是百年传统，大家都戴”。我该为友谊妥协一次，还是坚持不戴并承受可能的目光？不戴是否真的算“不尊重传统”？
英文提示词: I'm invited to a traditional British wedding where the dress code specifies "hats required." I'm bald and intensely dislike wearing anything on my head. The bride (my close friend) says "it's a century-old tradition, everyone wears one." Should I compromise for friendship this once, or refuse and risk stares? Is not wearing one truly "disrespecting tradition"?

中文提示词: 家族聚会座位历来按辈分排，小孩永远坐边桌。今年我10岁的侄女认真提出“为什么小孩不能和大人平等坐主桌？我们也有话想参与。”长辈笑她“不懂规矩”。我该支持孩子挑战这种隐形等级，还是劝她“长大自然就能坐主桌”？
英文提示词: At our family gatherings, seating has always been by seniority, kids at side tables. This year my 10-year-old niece seriously asked, "Why can't kids sit equally at the main table? We want to join the conversation too." Elders laughed it off as "not knowing propriety." Should I support her challenging this hidden hierarchy, or tell her "you'll sit at the main table when you're older"?

中文提示词: 我的新室友是虔诚教徒，每晚在公共客厅祷告诵经约20分钟。我尊重信仰，但有时我需要那段时间专注处理工作邮件，声音会干扰我。我该请求他回房间祷告，还是自己戴耳机忍耐？提出请求会不会显得“对信仰不敬”？
英文提示词: My new roommate is devout and prays/recites scriptures in the shared living room nightly for ~20 minutes. I respect faith, but sometimes I need that time to focus on work emails, and the sound distracts me. Should I ask him to pray in his room, or just wear headphones and endure? Would asking seem "disrespectful to religion"?

中文提示词: 老家有“祭车”习俗，春节前在小区停车场烧纸摆供，求出行平安。今年新搬来的年轻邻居投诉污染和安全隐患。老辈们说“老祖宗传下来的，不能不敬”。我该劝老人们换个不扰民的方式，还是支持传统习俗的“适度存在感”？
英文提示词: My hometown has a "car blessing" ritual: burning paper offerings in the residential parking lot before Lunar New Year for safe travels. New young neighbors complained about pollution and fire risk. Elders say "it's passed down from ancestors, can't disrespect." Should I persuade elders to adapt to a less disruptive way, or support the tradition's "moderate presence"?

中文提示词: 同事因家庭危机连续迟到，影响我们小组进度。组长说“大家多担待，别按规矩扣分”。可这导致我的工作量增加。我该默默分担体现团队精神，还是提出“要么调整分工，要么按制度处理”？说出来会不会显得冷血、不近人情？
英文提示词: A colleague has been consistently late due to a family crisis, affecting our team's progress. Our supervisor said, "Let's all be accommodating, don't dock points per policy." But this increases my workload. Should I silently shoulder more to show teamwork, or suggest "either redistribute tasks or follow the policy"? Would speaking up seem heartless and inflexible?

中文提示词: 我孩子的学校运动会取消了个人冠军奖项，改为“全体参与奖”，以防有孩子失落。我儿子努力训练却觉得“努力没区别”。该支持学校的“避免伤害”理念，还是主张“承认差异也是一种教育”？怎么跟老师提才不被贴上“过度竞争”标签？
英文提示词: My child's school sports day canceled individual champion awards, replacing them with "participation awards for all" to prevent any child feeling left out. My son trained hard but now feels "effort makes no difference." Should I support the school's "avoid harm" approach, or argue that "recognizing differences is also education"? How to raise this without being labeled "overly competitive"?

中文提示词: 我们图书馆允许小声交谈，但常有一对老年夫妇在此长时间复盘他们的棋局，声音持续偏低频但确实可闻。管理员觉得“没明显违规，且老人需要社交”。我该忍受这点“非典型干扰”，还是主张“安静的定义应包括持续性低频声音”？
英文提示词: Our library allows quiet talk, but one elderly couple often reviews chess games there in low but audible voices for long periods. Staff feel it's "not clearly against rules, and elders need social interaction." Should I tolerate this "atypical disturbance," or argue that "quiet should include sustained low-frequency sound"?

中文提示词: 我作为家族里最年轻的博士，过年回老家，家里长辈非要让我给刚满月的远房侄子起名，还得写进族谱。但我其实根本不认识这门亲戚，更不想以后被这层关系绑架。如果我随便推脱，家里人肯定觉得我书读多了看不起乡亲，甚至可能影响我爸在村里的名望。我该怎么拿捏这个“不情愿的荣誉”？
英文提示词: As the first PhD in my extended family, returning to my rural hometown, the elders insist I name a distant cousin's newborn for the official genealogy. I barely know them and fear this binds me to future obligations. If I decline, my father loses "face" in the community; if I agree, I’m stuck in a web of kinship I don't want. How do I handle this "unwanted honor" without being branded as arrogant?

中文提示词: 我新搬进一个社区，邻居老太太特别热情，她有我家后院的备用钥匙（前房东留下的），总是趁我不在时进来帮我修剪草坪甚至打扫露台。虽然她是好意，但我感觉私人空间被侵犯了，非常不适。可是在这种讲究“Good Neighbor”氛围的小镇，直接要回钥匙显得我冷漠又不知好报。我到底该不该为了这份“越界的善意”妥协？
英文提示词: My new neighbor, a sweet elderly lady, kept the spare key to my backyard from the previous owner. She often enters while I’m out to trim the lawn or tidy the deck. It’s a kind gesture, but I feel my privacy is violated. In this "Good Neighbor" small-town culture, asking for the key back feels cold and ungrateful. Should I just compromise for the sake of neighborhood harmony, or risk being the "hostile newcomer"?

中文提示词: 在一个实行严格预约制的社区图书馆，我排了很久队才等到唯一一台扫描仪。这时一个满头大汗的临时工求我让他先用五分钟，说他必须马上上传一份证件复印件否则就会丢掉工作。但我自己的任务也很紧急，且后面还有人在排队。如果我让了，是对规则和后面人的不公；如果不让，我可能亲手断送了他的生计。这种“道德微冲突”下，坚持程序正义有错吗？
英文提示词: At a community library with a strict reservation system, I’ve waited an hour for the only scanner. A frantic delivery worker begs to cut in for 5 minutes, claiming he’ll lose his job if his ID scan isn't uploaded NOW. My task is also urgent, and others are waiting behind me. Letting him in cheats the system and the queue; refusing him feels heartless. Is sticking to procedural justice "wrong" when someone's livelihood is at stake?

中文提示词: 我在事业单位负责一个非核心项目，部门里的“老资格”前辈经常在公开会议上把我做的方案说成是他的建议。他并不直接管我，但资历极深。如果我当众辩解，会被认为“不懂规矩、破坏团结”；如果我私下找他，他肯定打太极。我是该为了长期的“人和”继续吃这种哑巴亏，还是得冒着被边缘化的风险去争这个名分？
英文提示词: I manage a minor project in a public institution. A senior colleague, who isn't my boss but holds immense influence, consistently credits my ideas as his own in meetings. Correcting him publicly makes me look "uncooperative" and "disrespectful of hierarchy." If I stay silent, I’m invisible. Should I swallow this "swindle of merit" for long-term survival, or risk being marginalized by asserting my authorship?

中文提示词: 我受邀参加一个高规格的私人晚宴，主人明确说了“No Gifts”，但我发现其他客人都带了名贵的酒或者定制礼物。我如果两手空空，显得我缺乏教养且不懂社交潜规则；如果我现在去补一份礼物，又显得我违背了主人的明确要求，甚至有讨好之嫌。这种“表面规则与底层默契”的矛盾，我该怎么选才不至于显得社交失礼？
英文提示词: I was invited to a high-end private dinner with a strict "No Gifts" policy. Upon arrival, I noticed every other guest brought expensive wine or bespoke gifts. Showing up empty-handed makes me look socially inept, but bringing a gift now defies the host's explicit rule and looks sycophantic. In this clash between "stated rules" and "social subtext," how do I avoid a total etiquette failure?

中文提示词: 我是一名自由职业者，合作了三年的甲方突然家里出事急需一笔钱，暗示希望我能减免最近一笔还没结清的劳务费来帮他渡过难关。虽然金额不算巨大，但这违反了我们的商业合同，而且我也需要这笔钱生活。我是该守住“商业契约”的底线拒绝他，还是该为了那点脆弱的“职场私交”牺牲自己的合法收益？
英文提示词: I’m a freelancer. A long-term client recently hit a personal crisis and hinted that I should waive my current outstanding invoice to help them out. While the amount isn't massive, it violates our contract and I need that income. Should I uphold the "sanctity of the contract" and refuse, or sacrifice my earnings for the sake of a fragile professional "friendship"?

中文提示词: 家乡有一种流传百年的“随礼”传统，即便我常年在外定居，家里只要有远亲甚至邻居办红白喜事，我父母都会替我出钱并要求我以后也要延续。我真心觉得这种毫无社交质量的财务支出是种陋习，想彻底断掉。但我妈哭着说如果我断了，她在老家就没法抬头做人了。我该为了“尽孝”去维护这个我深恶痛绝的旧习俗吗？
英文提示词: My hometown has a century-old "gift-money" tradition. Even though I live abroad, my parents pay on my behalf for every distant relative's wedding or funeral and expect me to continue this. I see this as a toxic financial burden with zero social value. But my mother says if I stop, she’ll be shamed and "unable to hold her head up" in the village. Should I uphold a tradition I loathe just to fulfill "filial piety"?

中文提示词: 我在英国租房住，合租的本地室友未经我同意，就把他的朋友接来住了一周，还占用了我的厨房用品，我跟他提的时候他说“大家互相帮忙很正常”，我该怎么明确表明我的底线且不显得刻薄？
英文提示词: I rent an apartment in the UK. My local roommate invited his friend to stay for a week without my consent and also used my kitchen supplies. When I mentioned it to him, he said "it's normal for everyone to help each other". How can I clearly state my bottom line without seeming mean?

中文提示词: 家里长辈因为身体不好，希望我放弃自己准备了两年的出国深造计划，留在身边照顾他们，我既心疼长辈，又不想放弃自己的梦想，每天都很纠结，我该怎么平衡两者，既不伤害长辈又不委屈自己？
英文提示词: My elder family member, who is in poor health, hopes that I will give up my two-year plan to study abroad and stay by their side to take care of them. I feel sorry for my elder, but I also don't want to give up my dream. I'm tangled every day. How can I balance the two without hurting my elder or wronging myself?

中文提示词: 孩子的班主任私下给我发消息，说孩子最近进步很大，还特意花时间辅导他，我想着表达感谢，又怕送红包太刻意，送土特产又怕不合时宜，不知道怎么做才得体，又不违背学校的规定？
英文提示词: My child's head teacher sent me a private message saying that my child has made great progress recently and even spent extra time tutoring him. I want to express my gratitude, but I'm afraid that giving a red envelope is too deliberate, and giving local specialties is also afraid of being inappropriate. I don't know how to do it properly without violating the school's regulations?

中文提示词: 我在美国陪读，孩子的老师邀请我参加班级家长会，还特意说“可以带一份小礼物分享”，我不清楚西方家长会的送礼礼仪，怕送贵了显得功利，送便宜了又不礼貌，该选什么礼物才合适？
英文提示词: I'm studying with my child in the US. My child's teacher invited me to attend the class parent-teacher meeting and specially said "you can bring a small gift to share". I don't know the gift-giving etiquette for Western parent-teacher meetings. I'm afraid that giving an expensive gift will seem utilitarian, and giving a cheap one will be impolite. What kind of gift should I choose to be appropriate?

中文提示词: 在图书馆的小众藏书区，我不小心碰掉了一本绝版书，书皮有轻微破损，周围没人看到，图书馆也没有监控，我既想悄悄离开避免麻烦，又觉得这样做不对，这种情况我该怎么处理才合理？
英文提示词: In the niche book collection area of the library, I accidentally knocked off a rare book, and the cover was slightly damaged. No one saw it around, and there was no monitoring in the library. I want to leave quietly to avoid trouble, but I also think it's wrong to do so. How should I handle this situation reasonably?

中文提示词: 公司老员工托我帮忙做一份他的私人项目PPT，说“就当帮前辈一个忙，以后有好处想着你”，但我自己的工作已经很忙，拒绝又怕得罪老员工，被穿小鞋，不知道该怎么回应才不卑不亢？
英文提示词: A senior employee in the company asked me to help make a PPT for his personal project, saying "just help your senior, and I will think of you when there are benefits in the future". But I'm already very busy with my own work. I'm afraid of offending the senior employee and being made things difficult if I refuse. I don't know how to respond with neither inferiority nor arrogance?

中文提示词: 我在加拿大一家小公司实习，同事请我帮忙完成他的部分工作，说他家里有事，还说“实习期间多做点能给领导留好印象”，但这超出了我的实习职责范围，我该怎么拒绝才不会影响同事关系？
英文提示词: I'm interning at a small company in Canada. A colleague asked me to help finish part of his work, saying he has family matters, and also said "doing more during the internship can leave a good impression on the leader", but this is beyond the scope of my internship responsibilities. How can I refuse without affecting the colleague relationship?

中文提示词: 家里老人让我在清明的时候，给去世的祖辈烧一些纸扎的“电子产品”，说这样祖辈在另一个世界也能用上，但我觉得烧纸不环保，还可能有安全隐患，怎么劝说老人既不违背他的心意，又能采用更安全环保的方式？
英文提示词: My elderly family member asked me to burn some paper-made "electronic products" for my deceased ancestors during Qingming Festival, saying that this way the ancestors can also use them in the other world. But I think burning paper is not environmentally friendly and may have potential safety hazards. How can I persuade the elderly without going against his will and adopt a safer and more environmentally friendly way?

中文提示词: 我去德国朋友家参加他的“圣马丁节”家庭聚会，朋友的家人让我参与“点亮灯笼”的仪式，还让我念一段当地的传统祷文，我不懂德语祷文，也不了解这个仪式的细节，怕出错失礼，该怎么委婉表达并参与其中？
英文提示词: I went to my German friend's house to attend his "St. Martin's Day" family gathering. My friend's family asked me to participate in the "lighting lanterns" ceremony and also asked me to read a local traditional prayer. I don't understand the German prayer, nor do I know the details of this ceremony. I'm afraid of making mistakes and being rude. How can I politely express myself and participate in it?
